imports: from grok_web import get_client, BrowserWorkerPool, VideoGenerationResult, ImageGenerationResult, PostDetails, VideoMatchResult
config.note: Tests are async; generate.py does not emit async code. test_workflows.py is hand-written following these scenarios verbatim. Every scenario reflects the dict-based create_video({...}) API (v0.9.x).

scenarios.name: browse_favorite_unfavorite
scenarios.title: Browse gallery, favorite a post, verify, unfavorite
scenarios.user_problem: User browses their gallery, saves something to favorites to revisit later, then unsaves it.
scenarios.skip_reason: Requires real Chrome and a logged-in Grok account.

steps.description: list_posts returns PostSummary list with post IDs
get_post_details fetches full post record for the first returned post
favorite_post saves the post
list_posts(source=favorites) should now contain the post we just saved
unfavorite_post cleans up — un-stars the post so the test leaves no trace

scenarios.name: img2vid_roundtrip
scenarios.title: Generate video from an existing post and verify it appears as a child
scenarios.user_problem: User animates an existing Grok image post into a video and wants to confirm the video was registered under that parent.
scenarios.skip_reason: Creates a real video (consumes Grok credits).

steps.description: dict-based create_video with a post: reference runs the img2vid UI flow
get_post_details on the parent should list the new video as a child
delete_video cleans up

scenarios.name: upload2vid_retry_without_reupload
scenarios.title: Upload local frames once, retry generation cheaply via file: refs
scenarios.workflow: create_video(local files) -> capture image_file_ids -> create_video(images=['file:<id>',...]) -> delete_video x2 (cleanup)
scenarios.user_problem: User generates a video from local frames. The output gets moderated. They want to retry with the SAME uploaded frames instead of paying the upload cost again.
scenarios.skip_reason: Creates real videos (consumes Grok credits) and requires sample frame files.

steps.description: First pass: real upload + generation. image_file_ids captured from /rest/app-chat/upload-file responses.
Second pass: direct REST using 'file:' refs. No UI, no re-upload. Should be noticeably faster than the first call.
cleanup first video
cleanup second video

scenarios.name: upload_images_then_reuse
scenarios.title: Standalone upload_images API, then generate from the returned refs
scenarios.user_problem: User wants explicit control over upload lifecycle: upload once, then run N generations with different prompts on the same frames.
scenarios.skip_reason: Requires a prior UI-path create_video to prime the x-statsig-id cache (direct REST gate).

steps.description: Prime run — required so StatsigSnitch captures conversations/new and post/create sids.
upload_images returns list of 'file:<uuid>' strings ready to be passed to create_video
Generation that consumes the pre-uploaded refs via direct REST
cleanup prime
cleanup reused-refs video

scenarios.name: catch_post_render_moderation
scenarios.title: Generate, then verify Grok did NOT hide the video after rendering
scenarios.user_problem: Immediate NDJSON moderated=False is not the whole story — Grok runs a second moderation pass AFTER the video renders. User wants to catch that.
scenarios.skip_reason: Requires real Chrome and borderline content to exercise post-render moderation.

steps.description: Run a normal generation. gen.moderated reflects only the immediate NDJSON verdict.
Consult /rest/media/post/get: empty mediaUrl or placeholder-thumbnail means post-render moderation.
cleanup

scenarios.name: download_and_match_roundtrip
scenarios.title: Generate, download, then re-identify the post from the local file
scenarios.workflow: create_video -> download_video -> match_local_video -> verify round-trip -> delete_video (cleanup)
scenarios.user_problem: User has a locally-saved Grok video file and wants to find the post it came from — useful for reorganizers that sync local archives back to source posts.
scenarios.skip_reason: Creates a real video and writes it to a temp directory.

steps.description: Fresh video on the test post
Save the video locally for match step
match_local_video should map the local file back to the Grok post and video
cleanup

scenarios.name: pool_parallel_generation
scenarios.title: Run N generations in parallel via BrowserWorkerPool
scenarios.workflow: BrowserWorkerPool(2 workers) -> submit N jobs -> wait -> verify all completed
scenarios.user_problem: User has many camera commands to apply to the same source post and wants the wall-clock time to scale with worker count, not job count.
scenarios.skip_reason: Spawns multiple real Chromes. Expensive. Use --run-integration deliberately.

raw_code: async with BrowserWorkerPool(num_workers=2, max_retries=1, headless=True, close_chrome=True) as pool:
assert len(results) == len(ids), 'all jobs must finish'
assert len(workers_used) >= 2, 'pool should distribute across workers'
if r.success and r.data and r.data.get('video_id'):
steps.description: Run the three camera commands concurrently; both workers should pick up work, and any successful videos are cleaned up after.
